title_en: 4 Adaptations of The Odyssey Free to Stream Right Now If You Can’t Get Tickets to See Christopher Nolan’s $250 Million Epic
summary_en: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey lands in theaters in various formats this weekend, but what if your preferred format is already sold out? Well, you could stream one of these other notable Odysseus adaptations — FOR FREE! — if you can't get in to see Nolan’s film.
body_en:
Christopher Nolan ’s The Odyssey lands in theaters in various formats this weekend — read our review — but what if your preferred format is already sold out? Well, you could stream one of these other notable Odysseus adaptations — FOR FREE! — if you can't score tickets to see Nolan’s film.
Given that Homer’s epic poem is 3,000 years old, it’s obviously no surprise that Nolan’s movie isn’t the first stab at adapting the tale for the screen. Some projects only loosely adapted the poem — like O, Brother Where Art Thou? — but for the purposes of this article, we’re just showcasing projects that kept the saga’s mythic trappings in place.
Here then are three other Odyssey adaptations you can check out for free before or after you watch Nolan’s epic, and one that’s Odyssey-adjacent and is kinda sorta free.
Streaming for free on Kanopy .
Adapting the homestretch of The Odyssey, director Uberto Pasolini’s The Return is an exquisitely acted, demystified chronicle of Odysseus’s (a ripped Ralph Fiennes) homecoming after nearly two decades away. But this older, grizzled Odysseus is no arrogant trickster.
Perhaps he once was that clever warrior who duped the Trojans into accepting a giant wooden horse inside their city walls, but the man who finally washes ashore on Ithaca is haunted by PTSD and knows he’s committed every sin there is in the years he was away. Maybe the gods weren’t the reason he didn’t come home sooner. Maybe it was his own guilty conscience.
Featuring enthralling lead performances by Fiennes, Juliette Binoche as Penelope, Marwan Kenzari as Antinous, and Charlie Plummer as Telemachus, The Return is a brutal but beautiful interpretation of the final portion of The Odyssey. It also benefits from a barebones production and authentic Mediterranean locations, which enhances its realistic interpretation.
Streaming for free on Tubi .
Kirk Douglas is arguably the screen’s best Odysseus, perfectly capturing the iconic character’s arrogance, cleverness and action hero appeal — even if the movie he’s in is very much of its cheesy time. (Ulysses is the Latin version of Odysseus.)
Directed by Mario Camerini and produced by Dino De Laurentiis and Carlo Ponti, Ulysses is an Italian-language production made at the height of the sword & sandal era (this film predates Douglas’ Spartacus). Before Nolan cast Lupita Nyong’o in dual roles, Ulysses cast Silvana Mangano as both the steadfast wife Penelope and the witch Circe.
Ulysses is a truncated version of Homer’s The Odyssey, but even at such a short runtime the film keeps most of the classic set-pieces and basic narrative. The great Anthony Quinn plays the lead suitor Antinous, the character Robert Pattinson plays in Nolan’s version.
Streaming for free on Tubi .
Armand Assante makes for a fine mythic hero in this two-part TV miniseries adaptation produced by Francis Ford Coppola and Hallmark Entertainment and directed by Andrei Konchalovsky (Runaway Train, Tango & Cash). Greta Scacchi and Vanessa Williams also impress as Penelope and Calypso, respectively.
Produced during the early years of CGI, this Odyssey features visual effects on par with the best of Star Trek: The Next Generation but nowhere near what the Jurassic Park films had to play with. Nevertheless, The Odyssey is a fun and relatively faithful adaptation that sees Odysseus encounter the cyclops, Circe, Calypso, and gods like Hermes… while not just seeming like an episode of Xena or Hercules.
And finally…
Streaming with a free trial on Philo ; also available for rent or purchase digitally.
Hollywood’s last truly big screen attempt at adapting Homer before The Odyssey — Nolan was famously in the mix to direct it before opting to make Batman Begins instead — this Wolfgang Petersen-directed loose adaptation of The Iliad features some of the same characters who also appear in The Odyssey.
Troy cast Sean Bean as Odysseus, Diane Kruger as Helen of Troy, Brendan Gleeson as Menelaus, and Brian Cox as Agamemnon. For as much grief as Nolan’s film has received pre-release for historic inaccuracies and creative license , it’s worth noting how far Troy strayed from the classic source material and how many major changes it made.
Still, Troy is a very entertaining and sumptuous-looking take on the material featuring great turns by Brad Pitt, Eric Bana, Peter O’Toole and Orlando Bloom.
How many of these other Homer adaptations have you seen? And if you haven’t then will you seek them out on streaming? Let us know in the comments below.
